pattern: A node survives only if some root-to-leaf path through it reaches the limit, which a post-order recursion decides by pruning children first and dropping any node left with no children that was not itself a qualifying leaf.
hints: A leaf should stay only if the running sum from the root down to it is at least the limit.
For an internal node, prune each subtree against the remaining limit; if both children get pruned away, this node is on no sufficient path and goes too.
Recurse carrying limit minus the node's value; at a leaf keep it iff that remaining limit is non-positive, otherwise return None after pruning both children and dropping the node if both became None.

edge_inputs.description: single root meeting the limit survives
single root below the limit is pruned to empty
straight chain where the tail fails the limit

stress.description: random small binary trees serialized level-order with a random limit
stress.note: level-order serialization with random missing children; trailing nulls trimmed
